title: gamocarpha raffaellii
intro: Gamocarpha raffaellii is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Gamocarpha raffaellii can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and its leaves can also be applied topically as a poultice to speed up the healing process and reduce inflammation.
